close_reason: Positive observation: pinned-version rejection is correct
description:
src/lib.rs:343-357 rejects WritableSecretStore::put() when the URI was constructed with a pinned ?version=. GCP Secret Manager always creates a NEW version on put, so a pinned backend's get() would continue reading the old version forever — silently violating the WritableSecretStore::put 'durability' contract documented in secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1248-1252 ('a subsequent get on the same instance must return the written value').

A future contributor might 'simplify' this by removing the early-rejection ('it's just a runtime check; let GCP handle versioning'). That would re-introduce the silent-violation bug. The check must stay.

This is the only backend in the workspace that needs this guard (others either don't support put or don't have versioned reads). The pattern is unique and right.

Defense: the early-rejection enforces the durability invariant at the type-system-adjacent layer (URI parsing -> error). Removing it would defer the contract violation to silent runtime data loss.
title: Defend: pinned-version put() rejection prevents silent contract violation
